evals.name: preventive-ufw-docker-ci-runner
evals.prompt:
I'm setting up a Forgejo CI runner in Docker on my Ubuntu home server. The runner container needs to clone repos over SSH from the Forgejo bastion that runs directly on the host (port 22). My ufw is default-deny incoming with an allow rule for SSH from the LAN. Compose looks like this:

```yaml
services:
  ci-runner:
    image: forgejo/runner:6
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      FORGEJO_URL: https://forge.lan/
      FORGEJO_TOKEN: ${TOKEN}
```

Before I deploy, anything I should watch out for? Want to make sure the runner can actually reach the host's SSH.
evals.expected_output: Surface the ufw-docker bridge-drop pitfall. Explain that the container's source IP will be on a Docker bridge subnet (172.x.x.x), not on the LAN, so the existing ufw allow rule for LAN SSH won't apply. Recommend network_mode: host as the cleanest fix for an outbound-only container with no published ports. Optionally mention the alternatives (allow from bridge subnet, host.docker.internal). Should NOT just say 'add a ufw allow rule' or 'Docker bypasses ufw'.

evals.name: preventive-gluetun-qbit-sidecar
evals.prompt: Setting up gluetun + qBittorrent on my NAS using the standard `network_mode: service:gluetun` pattern. I plan to add an auto-recovery script that restarts gluetun if its healthcheck goes red. Anything dangerous about this setup I should know before I roll it out?
evals.expected_output: Surface the network_mode netns orphan pitfall. Explain that restarting gluetun without restarting qBittorrent will leave qBit in an orphaned netns — process keeps running, port mapping intact, but the namespace is empty. The auto-recovery script must restart children after the parent reaches healthy. Should mention SandboxID / `ip addr` as the diagnostic primitives. Should NOT just say 'looks fine' or 'gluetun is well-tested'.

evals.name: retrospective-git-script-intermittent
evals.prompt: Help me write up a post-mortem. My nightly auto-push script started failing every few days with 'error: cannot pull with rebase: You have unstaged changes'. Worked fine for weeks before. The script writes to a log file, then runs git pull --rebase, then commits and pushes. I need to document why and what to fix so this doesn't keep happening.
evals.expected_output: Drive the user through the post-mortem phases. Identify the sync-before-write pitfall — the script's order is write → pull-rebase, which fails when the tree is dirty AND origin has commits ahead. Explain why intermittent (only fires when both conditions hold). Recommend the structural fix: invert the order, sync before write. Reject stash-and-pop / --autostash as arg-tuning. Output should be a draft PM following a recognizable structure (What broke / Why / Fix / Verification / Open). Should NOT propose --autostash as the primary fix.

evals.name: retrospective-qbit-netns-orphan
evals.prompt: Yesterday around 18:30 my qBittorrent web UI suddenly stopped responding. `curl http://nas-01:8080/` returned 'Connection reset by peer'. `docker ps` showed gluetun (healthy) and qBit Up. Port mapping was fine. I spent two hours reading qBit's logs (which were normal) before someone on a forum suggested `docker restart qbittorrent` — that fixed it instantly. Help me write the PM so I understand what happened and add monitoring to catch it next time.
evals.expected_output: Walk the user through the PM phases. Identify this as the netns-orphan pitfall — gluetun must have restarted, leaving qBit's netns empty. Explain the diagnostic primitives (SandboxID, `docker exec qbit ip -4 addr` showing only `lo`). Recommend external HTTP probe of qBit's actual UI as the monitor (not just gluetun's healthcheck). Output should be a draft PM following the standard structure. Should NOT just say 'restart fixed it, mystery solved' — must explain the mechanism.
